overview: Imagen is Google's photorealistic image model — it thinks in photography terms. 'Taken on' and 'shot on' are powerful triggers. Color palette descriptions, lighting setups, and photography genre terms all work cleanly. Unlike Midjourney, full descriptive phrases work well (not just keywords). Practitioners report that Imagen excels at product and commercial photography briefs.

style.description: Photography style, genre, and aesthetic framing. 'Shot on' triggers strong photographic rendering.

lighting.description: Imagen reads both natural and studio lighting setups precisely.

camera_language.description: Camera body, lens type, film stock all significantly shift output.

color_palette.description: Color grade and palette descriptions shift the mood of the entire output.

subject_detail.description: Concrete physical description of subject, materials, textures.

power_patterns: [Photography genre] photograph of [subject description], [location/context], [lighting], [color palette], taken on [camera], [mood].
[Product/subject], [surface/background], [lighting direction and quality], [color grade], shot on [camera and lens], commercial photography.
antipatterns: Illustrated/painted/drawn — Imagen is a photorealistic model. Artistic style terms confuse it.
No lighting description — Imagen defaults to mediocre generic lighting without direction.
No camera reference — one of the fastest levers in Imagen and most prompts skip it entirely.

strong: Documentary photograph of an elderly fisherman repairing a net on a harbor dock at dawn. Natural overcast morning light. Warm skin tones against grey sky. Shot on Leica M10, 35mm. Quiet, dignified.

Product photograph: a ceramic coffee mug, earthy terracotta glaze, on a weathered oak surface. Single window light from left, soft shadow to the right. Warm morning palette. Shot on Hasselblad, 80mm macro. Commercial, clean.
weak: A nice photo of an old man fishing.
A coffee mug on a table.
